author: codex
scope: validator/rule-data triage only for next relation endpoint bucket after last materialized checkpoint plus applied-but-unmaterialized relation027; artifact-only prep; no materialize, validate, export-jsonl, provider-backed commands, generated canonical/wiki/DB edits, or relation-rule mutations

recommendation.summary: Do not widen implemented_by to accept entity subjects. After the last materialized checkpoint and after virtually excluding relation027's five applied-but-unmaterialized published_by claim->entity originals, the first top bucket is four implemented_by entity->entity rows, tied with operated_by entity->entity. The four implemented_by examples are not a coherent validator gap: two are same-entity self-links, one is a license-agreement agency/licensee row, and one is broad OCIP coverage context.
recommendation.recommended_action: Prepare a narrow relation cleanup batch that retires the four malformed implemented_by entity->entity submissions. Use replacement relations only where a row has a clear source-backed non-agency-role assertion; otherwise retire without replacement. Do not patch RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES.implemented_by.

replacement_guidance: For the LIRR/VPCT license row, a weak unshaped related_to entity->entity replacement may be acceptable if the cleanup preparer wants to preserve agency/licensee context.
For OCIP coverage, prefer no replacement unless a source-backed project/source carrier is already present in the same source; do not invent one in cleanup prep.
For the TBTA legal-name self-link, prefer no replacement because the entity payload already carries the legal-name fact.

why_not_validator_patch.reason: The current implemented_by shape intentionally models project -> entity. These rows do not show a stable entity-implemented-by-entity relation class; they mostly show self-links, legal-name/entity identity facts, license counterparties, or coverage context. Widening would hide extraction mistakes and conflict with the previous relation024/next5 decision not to broaden implemented_by for non-project subjects.

last_materialized_checkpoint_from_log.notes: The checkpoint materialized relation026's four has_timeline_event metric_claim->event retirements and supports event->metric_claim replacements.

applied_but_unmaterialized_adjustment.relation027: five published_by claim->entity originals were retired and five has_claim entity->claim replacements were appended while wave26 remained live; generated-output checks are pending.

checkpoint_context.method: Read-only bun --eval over readCanonicalRecords('data/canonical') and relationEndpointShapeIssue(), excluding only relation027 original record ids from data/review_notes/codex-relation-cleanup-batch027-prep.json. This is a simulated inventory over current canonical JSONL plus applied-but-unmaterialized durable cleanup, not a validate CLI run.

top_shape_buckets.classification: defer; also likely data cleanup, but next8 takes the first top bucket
defer; source object suggests containment/reference cleanup
defer; likely endpoint identity or relation-kind cleanup
defer; possible source-carried metric context, but lower count and needs separate source-page/metric semantics review
defer; relation025 precedent says claim-owned timeline rows are cleanup, not validator widening

exact_examples.triage: Same-entity self-link. Retire without replacement; the evidence is an attendance/representation heading, not an implementation relation.

evidence.text: AGENCY: MTA Long Island Rail Road ("LIRR") LICENSEE: VPCT Realty LLC ("VPCT")

exact_examples.triage: The source supports a license-agreement agency/licensee context, not implementation. If preserving it is useful, use a loose unshaped related_to entity->entity replacement; otherwise retire without replacement.

evidence.text: MTA Construction and Development identified 229 projects throughout the NYCT, LIRR, MNR and SBDP to be awarded within a 27-month period

exact_examples.triage: The evidence is OCIP package coverage/project population context, not NYCT being implemented by MTA. Retire unless a cleanup preparer finds an existing source-backed OCIP/project carrier in the same source.

evidence.text: The legal name of MTA Bridges and Tunnels is Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority.

exact_examples.triage: Same-entity self-link whose evidence is an identity/legal-name statement. Retire without replacement; the entity payload already carries this fact.

simulated_impact.assumption: All four original malformed implemented_by entity->entity submissions are retired. Replacement rows, if any, use unshaped or currently validator-compatible relation kinds and pass createSubmissionEntry/relationEndpointIssues guards. This artifact did not create replacements or edit retired.json/submissions.
simulated_impact.why_rule_patch_would_have_same_count_but: Adding entity as an implemented_by subject would also remove these four endpoint-shape issues, but would bless self-links and unrelated entity-to-entity context as implementation.

commands_run.outcome: Found existing relation rule/data prep artifacts through next7 and relation cleanup prep artifacts through batch027.
Observed existing shared worktree modifications, including relation rules/tests and relation027 durable cleanup files. No unrelated changes were reverted or overwritten.
Confirmed relation027 retired five published_by claim->entity originals and queued five has_claim entity->claim replacements behind wave26.
Read the last materialized checkpoint: relation026 was materialized and validate reported 68 unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape issues; relation027 is applied but unmaterialized.
Reviewed relation026 and relation027 cleanup bucket definitions and replacement shapes.
Reviewed next7 context; relation026 later materialized the metric_claim timeline cleanup that next7 recommended.
